channel.title: Cars & Bids: Auctions of cool cars, trucks, and SUVs
channel.description: Cars & Bids has daily auctions of cool enthusiast vehicles — like Porsche, BMW, Mercedes-Benz, and more.

image.title: Cars & Bids: Auctions of cool cars, trucks, and SUVs

item.title: 2024 Ferrari 296 GTS
item.description: THIS... is a 2024 Ferrari 296 GTS, finished in Bianco Cervino with a Charcoal interior. - The odometer currently displays approximately 600 miles. - The attached Carfax history report indicates no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Ferrari's past. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, this GTS cost $457,520 new. A partial list of notable factory equipment includes 20-inch forged wheels, carbon ceramic brakes with yellow calipers, a front suspension lift, Scuderia Ferrari shields on fenders, a power retractable hardtop, Daytona-style seats, carbon fiber interior trim, and an advanced front driving camera. The seller reports no notable modifications. - Unveiled in 2021 as the spiritual successor to the gorgeous Dino 246 of 1967-1974, Ferrari's 296 GTB (coupe) and GTS (folding hardtop convertible) pair a twin-turbo V6 with a single electric motor for a combined output of 818 horsepower. Styled in-house under Flavio Manzoni, the 296 is one of the prettiest mid-engined Ferraris in decades, and with a power-to-weight ratio of 560 hp/ton, the car demolishes the sprint to 0-60 mph in under 3 seconds and tops out north of 205 mph. - Power comes from a 3.0-liter twin-turbo V6 linked to a single-motor hybrid system, producing a combined output of 818 horsepower and 546 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via an 8-speed dual-clutch automatic transmission.

item.title: 1947 MG TC
item.description: THIS... is a 1947 MG TC, finished in green with a tan folding top and side curtains with a tan interior. - A partial list of notable currently installed equipment reported by the seller includes 19-inch wire wheels, a front fog lamp, dual SU carburetors, and a luggage rack. - Launched in 1945, the TC stood out as the first new MG released after World War II. It was closely related to the TB, which made its debut in 1939, but it benefited from several improvements including a wider body that gave the passengers more space. Suspension and electrical updates were also made to improve usability. The TC retired in 1949 after about 10,000 units were built. - Power comes from an XPAG 1250cc 4-cylinder engine, rated at about 54 horsepower. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 4-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2001 Toyota Tacoma PreRunner SR5 Stepside
item.description: THIS... is a 2001 Toyota Tacoma PreRunner SR5, finished in Black Sand Pearl with a gray interior. - The odometer on this Tacoma currently displays approximately 84,800 miles. - The attached Carfax history report contains a gap between 2001 and 2006, though it lists no mileage discrepancies for the duration of the report. It also shows registrations in California since new. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes 16-inch wheels, cloth upholstery, air conditioning, and more as detailed below. Notable modifications reported by the selling dealer are limited to a Glasstite tonneau cover. - Toyota split its global pickup range into two model lines when it released the original Tacoma in 1995. Developed for and built in America, it was a more leisure-oriented model than the utilitarian Hilux that soldiered on overseas—though the Tacoma still excelled at hard work. The lineup included 2- and 4-wheel-drive models, plus a regular or an extended cab. Production ended in 2005. - Power comes from a 2.7-liter inline-4, rated at 150 horsepower and 177 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 4-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2008 Dodge Viper SRT-10 Convertible
item.description: THIS... is a 2008 Dodge Viper SRT-10 Convertible, finished in Viper Very Orange with a black soft top and a black and gray interior. - The odometer on this Viper currently indicates approximately 8,500 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report lists no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Dodge's past. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes 18-inch front and 19-inch rear wheels, a limited-slip differential, suede and leather upholstery, and power-adjustable pedals. - Notable modifications reported by the seller include an APR Performance rear wing, an APR Performance front lip, a hood stripe, and a Pioneer head unit. - Dodge's fourth-generation Viper hatched for the 2008 model year with a familiar-looking exterior design and several significant changes underneath the sheet metal. It gained a slightly bigger and heavily revised engine, a side-exit exhaust system developed to reduce heat levels in the cabin, and a retuned suspension system. Production ended in 2010. - Power comes from an 8.4-liter V10, rated at 600 horsepower and 560 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2006 Pontiac Solstice
item.description: THIS... is a 2006 Pontiac Solstice, finished in Cool with a black soft top and a Steel and Sand interior. - This Solstice is equipped with a 5-speed manual transmission, and the odometer currently indicates approximately 42,500 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report lists no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Pontiac's past. - A partial list of notable equipment reported by the seller includes the Convenience, Premium, and Power packages, 18-inch wheels, and air conditioning. The seller reports no notable modifications. - Long after the Firebird's departure left GM's performance division without a sports car, the Solstice hit Pontiac showrooms in 2006. Looking nearly identical to the concept car of 2002 and riding on GM's rear-wheel drive Kappa platform, the Solstice delivered the driving excitement its dynamic styling promised in either coupe or convertible form. Despite its potential as an American Miata competitor, production ended in 2010 with the discontinuation of the Pontiac division. - In a clever marketing ploy, Pontiac famously teamed up with the reality competition show The Apprentice to introduce the Solstice through a competition in which two teams were tasked with creating a promotional brochure that was later presented to Pontiac executives. Pontiac ran a 60-second ad during the episode that directed viewers to their website, where the specially badged “First 1,000” units could be pre-ordered. Traffic on the site increased 1,393% just 15 minutes after the ad, and in 41 minutes the "First 1,000" units were sold out. - Power comes from a 2.4-liter inline-4, rated at 177 horsepower and 166 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 5-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2020 Subaru BRZ Limited
item.description: THIS… is a 2020 Subaru BRZ Limited, finished in Crystal White Pearl with a black interior. - This BRZ features the desirable 6-speed manual transmission. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report contains a reporting gap from 2020 to 2024, though it shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies during the given time period. It also indicates registration to one owner in California since new. - According to the window sticker in the gallery, this BRZ is a Limited model, and notable factory equipment includes the Performance Package, the Popular Package #2, a Torsen limited-slip rear differential, and heated front seats. - A full list of notable modifications reported by the seller is detailed below. Highlights include a Borla cat-back exhaust, Annex Suspension coilovers, SPC rear lower control arms, and an STI aero kit. - Developed jointly by Toyota and Subaru, the first-generation BRZ was introduced for the 2013 model year. It was instantly celebrated as a reasonably affordable coupe with excellent handling. It remained in production with a Subaru-sourced naturally-aspirated flat-4 engine until late 2020, when it passed the torch to the second-generation model. - Power comes from a 2.0-liter flat-4, rated at 205 horsepower and 156 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2020 Toyota Land Cruiser Heritage Edition
item.description: THIS… is a 2020 Toyota Land Cruiser Heritage Edition, finished in Midnight Black Metallic with a black interior. - The attached Carfax history report contains a reporting gap from 2020 to 2023, though it shows no mileage discrepancies during the given time period. - According to the iSeeCars window sticker provided in the gallery, this Land Cruiser is a Heritage Edition model, and notable factory equipment includes a Torsen limited-slip differential with locking feature, power sunroof, heated steering wheel, rear heated seats, and more as detailed below. - A list of notable modifications reported by the seller is detailed below. Highlights include Rigid Industries D2 Side Shooter LED ditch lights, a Dissent Off-Road aluminum roof rack, and an aftermarket cargo area hard plate. - Toyota's 200-Series Land Cruiser arrived for 2008 with a more powerful V8 shared with the Tundra, a longer list of technology added to increase on-road comfort and off-road prowess, and a quieter interior. It retained rugged body-on-frame construction, but put an even greater emphasis on luxury than its predecessor. Toyota made several updates to the 200-Series before production ended in 2021. - Power comes from a 3UR-FE 5.7-liter V8 engine, rated at 381 horsepower and 401 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels via an 8-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2012 Subaru Impreza WRX STI Hatchback
item.description: THIS... is a 2012 Subaru Impreza WRX STI Hatchback, finished in WR Blue Pearl with a Carbon Black interior. - This WRX STI features a 6-speed manual transmission. - The attached Carfax history report indicates no mileage discrepancies in this WRX STI’s past. - This Impreza is a WRX STI Hatchback model. A partial list of notable equipment reported by the seller includes 18-inch wheels, front and rear limited-slip differentials, a Driver-Controlled Center Differential (DCCD), Subaru Intelligent Drive (SI-Drive), and heated front seats. - Notable modifications reported by the seller include a replacement turbocharged 2.5-liter flat-4 engine, a COBB Tuning air intake system, ECU tune, and downpipe, PowerStop brakes, HELLA Supertone horns, and an aftermarket gauge cluster trim panel with DEFI gauges. - Power comes from a 2.5-liter turbocharged flat-4. Output is sent to all four wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission and Subaru's Symmetrical All-Wheel Drive system.

item.title: 2000 BMW 528i Sedan
item.description: THIS... is a 2000 BMW 528i Sedan, finished in Biarritz Blue with a gray interior. - This BMW features the desirable 5-speed manual transmission. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report indicates no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this 528i's past. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes the Sport Package, a power sunroof, and heated front seats. - Notable modifications reported by the seller are limited to LED headlights, a ZHP shift knob, and a Megan Racing strut tower brace. - BMW introduced the E39-generation 5 Series to the North American market for the 1997 model year. It wore a more modern design than its predecessor, and it received aluminum suspension components to keep weight in check. The inline-6 powered 528i sedan was offered from 1997 through 2000. It was replaced by the 530i for the 2001 model year. - Power comes from a 2.8-liter straight-6 engine, rated at 193 horsepower and 206 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels through a 5-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 1999 Nissan Silvia
item.description: THIS… is a 1999 Nissan Silvia, finished in Silver with a black interior. - This is a right-hand drive, Japanese-market coupe that's titled in Florida. It's equipped with a 6-speed manual transmission and a metric instrument cluster, and its odometer currently displays about 129,400 kilometers, which represents approximately 80,400 miles. - A partial list of notable factory equipment reported by the seller includes cloth upholstery, power-operated windows, power-adjustable mirrors, and air conditioning. - Notable modifications reported by the seller are extensive, and the full list is detailed below. Highlights include SR20DET and 6-speed manual swaps, a Haltech ECU, Blitz coilovers, and 18-inch aftermarket wheels. - Released in Japan in 1999, and never officially sold in the United States, the S15-generation Silvia was slightly smaller than its predecessor and it was characterized by a sharper-looking exterior design. Rear-wheel drive was the only configuration offered, and buyers could choose between a naturally aspirated and a turbocharged engine. Production ended in 2002, and Nissan hasn't used the Silvia nameplate since. - Power comes from a swapped SR20DET 2.0-liter turbocharged 4-cylinder, rated at about 247 horsepower and 203 lb-ft of torque in stock form. Due to the modifications performed to this Nissan, it may produce more power, but a dyno sheet was not provided to confirm. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 1985 Mercedes-Benz Unimog U1300L
item.description: THIS... is a 1985 Mercedes-Benz Unimog U1300L, finished in camouflage with a brown interior. - This Unimog is a left-hand-drive, European-market truck that was reportedly built for the West German military. It's titled in South Carolina and it's equipped with a metric instrument cluster, and the 5-digit odometer currently displays about 64,800 kilometers or approximately 40,300 miles. - A partial list of notable equipment reported by the seller includes portal axles, locking front, center, and rear differentials, folding bedsides, and vinyl upholstery. Notable modifications reported by the seller include 20-inch wheels and Dialight and J.W. Speaker LED headlights. - Mercedes-Benz built the heavy-duty 435-Series Unimog from 1975 to 1993. The model was available in numerous configurations, including different cab styles, and it could be outfitted for just about any job. Some 435s were used by construction companies, others worked in logging, while the U1300L often enlisted in the West Germany military. Against a great many odds, the 435-Series Unimog even has an impressive racing pedigree: It took first place in the 1985 Paris-Dakar Rally's truck category. - Power comes from a 5.7-liter diesel straight-6, rated at about 128 horsepower and 268 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear or all four wheels via an 8-speed manual transmission and a 2-speed transfer case.

item.title: 2010 Audi S4 Prestige
item.description: THIS… is a 2010 Audi S4 Prestige, finished in Ibis White with a black interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no mileage discrepancies in this S4’s past. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes the Driver Assist package, adaptive dampers, a sports rear differential, a sunroof, Nappa leather upholstery, power-adjustable heated front seats, and a Bang & Olufsen premium sound system. - Notable modifications reported by the seller include an Integrated Engineering Stage 1 ECU and TCU tunes, an Integrated Engineering cold-air intake, a Solo Performance exhaust system with downpipe baffles, 034Motorsport lowering springs, aftermarket high-carbon iron brake rotors, EBC Redstuff brake pads, and 20-inch Niche M147 Essen wheels. - Called B8 internally, the fourth-generation Audi A4 arrived for 2009, and the quicker S4 made its debut for 2010. While it surfed the downsizing wave by swapping its predecessor's V8 for a V6, it offered enthusiasts more torque and quicker acceleration. Engineers gave the Quattro system a rear bias for better handling, and the S4 reflected Audi's upmarket push by offering luxury and tech in spades. - Power comes from a 3.0-liter supercharged V6, rated at 333 horsepower and 325 lb-ft of torque in stock form. Due to the modifications performed to this S4, it may produce more power, but a dyno sheet was not provided to confirm. Output is sent to all four wheels via a 7-speed dual-clutch automatic transmission and Audi’s permanent Quattro all-wheel-drive system.

item.title: 1966 Chevrolet Impala Super Sport Coupe
item.description: THIS... is a 1966 Chevrolet Impala Super Sport Coupe, finished in blue with a brown interior. - This coupe's factory-fitted engine has been replaced by a 350-cubic-inch V8, according to the seller. - A partial list of notable currently installed equipment reported by the seller includes an aluminum radiator, front disc brakes, 20-inch wheels, custom upholstery, a Pioneer touchscreen, and an aftermarket sound system. - Chevrolet launched the fourth-generation Impala for the 1965 model year. The model was one of the brand's best-selling cars, and it was available in a dizzying number of configurations ranging from a family-friendly nine-seater wagon to a sporty two-door convertible. Chevrolet offered both six- and eight-cylinder engines, though the vast majority of buyers opted for the latter. Production ended in 1970, but the Impala nameplate stuck around until the 10th-generation model retired in 2020. - Power comes from a 350-cubic-inch V8. Output is sent to the rear wheels via an automatic transmission.

item.title: 2019 Chevrolet Colorado Z71 4x4
item.description: THIS... is a 2019 Chevrolet Colorado Z71 4x4, finished in Pacific Blue Metallic with a Jet Black interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no mileage discrepancies in this Chevrolet’s past. It also shows that this Colorado has been registered to one owner in Tennessee since new. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, this Colorado is a Z71 4x4 model, and notable factory equipment includes the Trailering Equipment Package, 17-inch Dark Argent Metallic cast aluminum wheels, a Z71 off-road suspension system, a locking rear differential, heated front seats, and an 8-inch color touchscreen. - Notable modifications reported by the seller are limited to black Chevrolet badge wheel center caps and a blue Z71 badge. - Chevrolet released the second-generation Colorado in North America for 2015. Closely related to the GMC Canyon, the Colorado was offered in several variants ranging from a bare-bones work truck to an upmarket highway cruiser. Buyers also had several cab configurations to choose from. Sales of the Colorado in North America ended after the 2022 model year. - Power comes from a Duramax 2.8-liter turbodiesel inline-4, rated at about 181 horsepower and 369 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear or all four wheels via a 6-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 1991 Suzuki Jimny 4x4
item.description: THIS... is a 1991 Suzuki Jimny 4x4, finished in beige with a black and gray interior. - This Jimny is a Japanese-spec, right-hand-drive Kei SUV that's titled in Virginia and located in California. It's equipped with a 5-speed manual transmission and a metric instrument cluster, and the 5-digit odometer shows about 58,100 kilometers, or roughly 36,200 miles. - A partial list of notable equipment reported by the selling dealer includes 15-inch wheels, manual locking front hubs, and manual side windows. - Notable modifications reported by the selling dealer include an HKS air filter, a suspension lift, an open-roof conversion with roll cage, tubular bumpers, LED headlights and taillights, an LED light bar, diamond-plate lining on the exterior and interior door panels, NA Miata seats, and a wood-grain steering wheel. - Introduced in 1970 (initially with a 2-stroke engine), the Jimny has long offered folks in dense urban environments a capable 4x4 that easily fits within the restricted confines of a large city. The 2nd-generation model was released in 1981, and a 1990 update brought a larger 657cc F6A engine, an intercooler, and revised suspension. Production continues today with the fourth-generation model. - Power comes from a 657cc turbocharged inline-3, rated at about 58 horsepower and 64 lb-ft of torque. Output is routed to the rear or all four wheels via a 5-speed manual transmission and a 2-speed transfer case.

item.title: 1989 Mini Cooper
item.description: THIS... is a 1989 Mini Cooper, finished in Bugatti Blue with a black interior, and offered by MotorcarsOfTheMainLine, a Cars & Bids Certified Seller. - This Mini is a European-market, left-hand drive coupe currently titled and located in Pennsylvania. It's equipped with a 4-speed manual transmission and a metric instrument cluster, and the five-digit odometer displays about 40,800 kilometers, which represents approximately 25,400 miles. - The attached Carfax history reports starts in 2023, though it lists no accidents or mileage discrepancies for the duration of the report. - A partial list of notable currently installed equipment reported by the selling dealer includes a 1000cc built engine, a double carburetor setup, a 4-into-1 sport exhaust system, 12-inch wheels, a custom suspension with upgraded shocks, sway bar, and adjustable trailing arms, cross-drilled and slotted disc brakes, Bugatti Blue exterior paint with black stripes, Sport Pack fender flares, an LED light conversion, black leather bucket seats with matching piping, brushed aluminum dashboard with full gauges, and a JVC touchscreen head unit. - First marketed as the Austin Seven and the Morris Mini-Minor, the original Mini made its debut in 1959 as an alternative to the bubble cars that were common on British roads during the 1950s. It was an extremely innovative four-seater, thanks in part to a transversally-mounted engine (a rare layout at the time) and front-wheel drive. Every attempt to replace the Mini was unsuccessful, and the model remained in production with only minor changes until 2000. About 5.4 million units were built. - Power comes from a 1000cc liter inline-4 engine. Output is sent to the front wheels via a 4-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2017 Mercedes-Benz G550
item.description: THIS... is a 2017 Mercedes-Benz G550, finished in Palladium Silver Metallic with a black interior. - The attached Carfax history report lists no accidents in this G550's past. - A build sheet is provided in the gallery, and a partial list of notable equipment reported by the seller includes locking front, center, and rear differentials, a front brush guard, heated, ventilated, and power-adjustable front seats, heated rear seats, and a Harman Kardon surround sound system. The only notable modifications reported by the seller are a starlight headliner and aftermarket ambient lighting. - Mercedes-Benz joined forces with Steyr-Daimler-Puch to create the original G-Class (W460). It was released in 1979 and aimed largely at farmers and adventurers; Beverly Hills wasn't on the company's radar yet. The G's upmarket ascent started when the extensively updated W463 arrived in 1990. It looked a lot like the original G, but it gained a longer list of features and a permanent four-wheel-drive system. Civilian production ended in 2018. - Power comes from a 4.0-liter twin-turbocharged V8, rated at 416 horsepower and 450 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the four wheels via a 7-speed automatic transmission and a 2-speed transfer case.

item.title: 2009 Lexus GS 460
item.description: THIS… is a 2009 Lexus GS 460, finished in Matador Red Mica with a black interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no mileage discrepancies in this GS 460's past. It also shows that this Lexus has been registered to two owners since new. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes 18-inch alloy wheels, a power sunroof, leather upholstery, heated front seats, and a Mark Levinson sound system. The seller reports no notable modifications. - The Lexus GS is the brand's mid-sized luxury sedan competitor to the Mercedes-Benz E-Class and BMW 5 Series. The third-generation GS was introduced for the 2006 model year and given a round of updates in 2008. The GS was offered as the 6-cylinder GS 350, and the 8-cylinder GS 460, and only 56 U.S.-market GS 460s were reportedly built for the 2009 model year. - Power comes from a 4.6-liter V8, rated at 342 horsepower and 339 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via an 8-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2001 Ford Mustang SVT Cobra Convertible
item.description: THIS... is a 2001 Ford Mustang SVT Cobra Convertible, finished in Silver Metallic with a black soft top and a Dark Charcoal interior. - This Mustang is equipped with a 5-speed manual transmission, and the odometer currently displays approximately 45,100 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Ford’s past. - A partial list of notable equipment reported by the seller includes SVT handling suspension, a Traction-Lok limited-slip differential, a power-folding soft top, and a Mach 460 radio. - Notable modifications reported by the seller are limited to 17-inch aftermarket wheels, a JLT intake, and a front grille Cobra badge. - The 2001 SVT Cobra was developed by Ford's Special Vehicle Team as a high-performance version of the Mustang, featuring enhancements over the standard model including independent rear suspension and upgraded braking and handling components. The SVT Cobra convertible offered open-air driving combined with exclusive SVT design and engineering elements. It remains a notable entry in the Mustang lineage for its balanced performance and collectible potential among enthusiasts. - Power comes from a 4.6-liter V8, rated at 320 horsepower and 317 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 5-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2025 Porsche Cayenne S E-Hybrid Coupe
item.description: THIS... is a 2025 Porsche Cayenne S E-Hybrid Coupe, finished in Paint-to-Sample Deep Green Metallic with a black interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Porsche’s past. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes the Premium Package Plus, 21-inch RS Spyder wheels finished in Neodyme, Exclusive Design taillights, sport taillights in Dark Bronze, brake calipers in high gloss black, leather upholstery, and a heated leather GT Sport steering wheel. The seller reports no notable modifications. - The third-generation Cayenne debuted for the 2019 model year, with the E-Hybrid variant offering a blend of performance and improved efficiency. The Cayenne S E-Hybrid Coupe combines a plug-in hybrid powertrain with coupe-inspired styling, enhancing both driving dynamics and practicality. This model continues Porsche's tradition of offering advanced technology and luxury features in the SUV segment. - Power comes from a gasoline-electric plug-in hybrid drivetrain, which consists of a 3.0-liter turbocharged V6, an electric motor, and a lithium-ion battery. The system is rated at a combined 512 horsepower and 553 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels via an 8-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2015 BMW 228i Convertible
item.description: THIS... is a 2015 BMW 228i Convertible, finished in Glacier Silver Metallic with a black soft top and a black interior, and offered by GreatNeckAutoClub, a Cars & Bids Certified Seller. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report indicates no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this BMW’s past. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes the Sport Line trim, leather upholstery, and heated front seats. The selling dealer reports no notable modifications. - BMW released the 2 Series for the 2014 model year as a replacement for the two-door variants of the 1 Series. At launch, the range consisted of the 228i, which received a 4-cylinder engine, and the M235i, which was powered by a straight-6. Both picked up where their esteemed predecessors left off by offering enthusiasts balanced handling, strong performance, and the luxurious interior BMW is known for. - Power comes from a 2.0-liter turbocharged inline-4, rated at 240 horsepower and 255 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via an 8-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2022 Audi SQ8 Prestige
item.description: THIS... is a 2022 Audi SQ8 Prestige, finished in Mythos Black Metallic with a black interior. - The attached Carfax history report indicates no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this SQ8's past. It also shows registration to one owner since new. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, this SQ8 is a Prestige model, and notable factory equipment includes the Prestige, Sport, and Black Optic packages, a panoramic sunroof, and a Bang & Olufsen sound system. The seller reports no notable modifications. - Audi released the SQ8 for 2020. The model was positioned between the base Q8 and the range-topping RSQ8, and it lived up to this positioning with a twin-turbocharged V8, model-specific styling, and a longer list of standard features. Although it weighs over 5,000 pounds, the SQ8 takes 3.6 seconds to reach 60 mph from a stop thanks in part to standard Quattro all-wheel-drive. Production continues today. - Power comes from a 4.0-liter twin-turbocharged V8, rated at 500 horsepower and 568 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels via an 8-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2003 Ford Focus SVT
item.description: THIS... is a 2003 Ford Focus SVT, finished in Vermillion Red with a black and red interior. - This Focus features a 6-speed manual transmission. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no mileage discrepancies in this Ford’s past. - A partial list of notable equipment reported by the seller includes 17-inch wheels, an SVT-specific body kit, front bucket seats, and air conditioning. - Notable modifications reported by the seller include a DC Sports intake system, an FSWerks cat-back exhaust, Raceland coilovers, and a JL Audio subwoofer. - Introduced in 2002, the Focus SVT offered an exclusive 6-speed manual transmission, a Cosworth-tuned twin-cam four-cylinder, a special body kit, and unique suspension tuning. Hailed by the press as one the best-handling front-wheel drive cars of the era, the Focus SVT remains an outstanding choice for fans of Euro-flavored hot hatches more than two decades on. - Power comes from a 2.0-liter 4-cylinder engine, rated at 170 horsepower and 145 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the front wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2018 BMW 440i xDrive Coupe
item.description: THIS... is a 2018 BMW 440i xDrive Coupe, finished in Black Sapphire Metallic with a Night Blue interior. - This BMW is a Canadian-market coupe that was imported and declared to meet US highway safety specifications in April 2022. It's equipped with a digital instrument cluster. - The attached Carfax history report lists no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this 440i's past. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes the M Sport, Premium, and Driving Assistance packages, 19-inch Style 704M wheels, a power sunroof, and a heated steering wheel. - Notable modifications reported by the seller include a protuningfreaks bootmod3 Stage 2+ ECU tune, an xHP Stage 2 transmission tune, and XPEL Stealth paint protection film. - BMW released the first 4 Series for the 2014 model year to replace the two-door 3 Series. Turning the two-door into a standalone model allowed engineers to lower and widen it relative to the F30-generation 3 Series it was related to, while fitting a sportier suspension system. At launch, the range included two models called 428i and 435i, respectively. They were replaced by the 430i and the 440i for 2017 as part of a mid-cycle update that brought more powerful engines across the range and a new iDrive infotainment system, among other changes. - Power comes from a 3.0-liter turbocharged inline-6, rated at 320 horsepower and 330 lb-ft of torque in stock form. Due to the modifications performed to this 440i, it may produce more power, but a dyno sheet was not provided to confirm. Output is sent to all four wheels via an 8-speed automatic transmission and BMW's xDrive permanent all-wheel drive system.

item.title: 2006 Bentley Continental GT
item.description: THIS... is a 2006 Bentley Continental GT, finished in Umbrian Red with a Saffron and Hotspur interior. - The odometer on this Continental GT currently displays approximately 49,000 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no mileage discrepancies in this Bentley’s past. - A partial list of notable equipment reported by the seller includes 19-inch Mulliner wheels, a self-leveling air suspension system, heated front seats with memory, a Breitling clock on the dashboard, and front and rear parking sensors. Notable modifications reported by the seller are limited to an Alpine head unit. - The Bentley Continental GT was unveiled in 2003 for the 2004 model year. It was Bentley's first car to launch under the ownership of the Volkswagen Group, and it allowed Bentley to mass-produce the Continental GT, reaching a wider customer base upon launch. Journalists praised the new Continental GT for its build quality, comfort, and performance. - Power comes from a twin-turbocharged 6.0-liter W12, rated at 552 horsepower and 479 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels via a 6-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2008 BMW X3 3.0si
item.description: THIS... is a 2008 BMW X3 3.0si, finished in Black Sapphire Metallic with a black interior. - This X3 is equipped with the desirable 6-speed manual transmission. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report indicates no mileage discrepancies in this X3’s past. It also shows Western and Southern ownership since new. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes a panoramic sunroof, leather upholstery, heated front and rear seats, and automatic climate control. - Notable modifications reported by the seller include 19-inch Style 191M wheels, LED halos in the headlights, and a navigation system with wireless Apple CarPlay and wired Android Auto. BMW released the first X3 for 2004 as a smaller, lighter, and more affordable alternative to the X5. Built in Austria and called the E83 internally, it landed with 6-cylinder power, a new version of BMW's all-wheel-drive system, and suspension components that traced their roots to the 3 Series. It was immensely popular and it helped accelerate the proliferation of SUVs across the BMW range. - Power comes from an N52 3.0-liter inline-6, rated at 260 horsepower and 225 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission and BMW's xDrive permanent all-wheel drive system.

item.title: 2025 Porsche Macan Turbo Electric
item.description: THIS... is a 2025 Porsche Macan Turbo Electric, finished in Ice Grey Metallic with a Bordeaux Red and black interior. - The odometer on this Macan currently displays approximately 1,500 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Macan’s past. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, this Macan cost $121,855 new. A partial list of notable factory equipment includes the Premium and Sport Chrono packages, 22-inch RS Spyder Design wheels, high-gloss black exterior accents, heated front Comfort seats, an Augmented Reality head-up display, and Porsche Electric Sport Sound. The seller reports no notable modifications. - Introduced for the 2024 model year, the Porsche Macan Turbo Electric marks a fully electric evolution of the Macan, built on the Premium Platform Electric (PPE) chassis and producing roughly 200 more horsepower than its predecessor to offset increased weight. It features a sleeker, more aerodynamic design with a sloping rear roofline and a redesigned front end with lower-set headlights. It also brings in advanced tech like an augmented reality head-up display, while air suspension allows adjustable ride height. The “Turbo” designation now reflects its place at the top of the lineup rather than the presence of a turbocharged engine. - Power comes from two electric motors that draw electricity from a 100-kilowatt-hour lithium-ion battery pack to power all four wheels with about 630 horsepower and 833 lb-ft of torque. In 2025, the Macan Turbo Electric received a maximum driving range rating of 293 miles from the EPA.

It stood out from its predecessor with a more compact size, revised styling that did away with the pop-up headlights of the previous generation, and a notable bump in horsepower. Interior materials were improved, as were performance metrics; the C6 was sold in the United States until 2013. - Power comes from a 6.0-liter LS2 V8, rated at 400 horsepower and 400 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2017 Maserati Levante

The seller reports no notable modifications. - Aimed at the Porsche Cayenne, the Levante made its debut for 2017 with muscular-looking styling, a luxurious interior, and a selection of six- and eight-cylinder engines. It took Maserati into the SUV segment for the first time, but it was developed with a focus on on-road performance rather than off-road prowess. Production ended in 2024. - Power comes from a 3.0-liter twin-turbocharged V6, rated at 345 horsepower and 369 lb-ft of torque. item.title: 2010 BMW 135i Coupe
item.description: THIS… is a 2010 BMW 135i Coupe, finished in Le Mans Blue Metallic with a black interior. - This 135i features the desirable 6-speed manual transmission. - The attached Carfax history report contains a reporting gap from 2016 to 2022, though it shows no mileage discrepancies during the given time period. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes the M Sport Package, a power sunroof, and automatic climate control. - Notable modifications reported by the seller include a Quaife limited-slip differential, 18-inch Apex ML-10RT forged wheels, and OEM Euro LCI headlights. - Marketed as a modern take on the vaunted 2002, the 1 Series coupe was introduced for the 2008 model year. It arrived as a rear-wheel-drive four-seater that was smaller, lighter, and more affordable than the 3 Series coupe. Available models included the entry-level 128i and the more powerful 135i, both of which are sought after for their nimble handling characteristics. With a potent turbocharged inline-6, the petite 135i quickly became a popular platform to modify. - Power comes from an N54 3.0-liter twin-turbocharged straight-6, rated at 300 horsepower and 300 lb-ft of torque in stock form. Due to the modifications performed to this BMW, it may produce more power, but a dyno sheet was not provided to confirm. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission.

It was sold as the FX for the 2009 to 2013 model years in the U.S. before being renamed the QX70. The Sport Package was only available on the V8-powered FX50, and models that were so equipped earned an "S" in their model designation. - Power comes from a VK50VE 5.0-liter V8, rated at 390 horsepower and 369 lb-ft of torque. Based on the third-generation hatchback, it arrived with a muscular-looking body kit, torque-vectoring all-wheel drive, a drift mode, and a turbocharged EcoBoost engine. Production ended in 2018, and Ford hasn't released another Focus RS for the U.S. market since. - Power comes from a turbocharged 2.3-liter inline-4, rated at 350 horsepower and 350 lb-ft of torque. item.title: 2011 Porsche Cayman S
item.description: THIS... is a 2011 Porsche Cayman S, finished in Basalt Black Metallic with a black interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Porsche’s past. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, this Cayman is an S model, and notable factory equipment includes Sport Chrono Package Plus, 18-inch wheels, a limited-slip differential, heated seats, and a Bose sound system. - Notable modifications reported by the seller include Fabspeed carbon fiber dual exhaust tips, a Racing Dynamics carbon fiber strut tower brace, a Porsche SportDesign steering wheel, and an Alcantara headliner. - Porsche introduced the Cayman for 2006 and immediately threw the automaker's entire hierarchy into question. Taking the Boxster's compact mid-engine layout and adding a fixed roof to the chassis made the 987 Cayman the most balanced Porsche available, and engineers were forced to keep engine output low enough to not threaten the 911 Carrera's dominance. The 987.2 further enhanced the Cayman's appeal in 2009 with more powerful engines and the elimination of the intermediate shaft (IMS) bearing. - Power comes from a 3.4-liter flat-6, rated at 320 horsepower and 273 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 7-speed PDK dual-clutch automatic transmission.

In America, the 300ZX was offered between the 1990 and 1996 model years. - Power comes from a twin-turbocharged 3.0-liter V6, rated at 300 horsepower and 283 lb-ft of torque. It was envisioned as a daily-drivable roadster, so it offered four seats and amenities not often associated with past models, such as a conventional navigation system. It was also Ferrari's first standard production model to feature a dual-clutch transmission, a power-folding hardtop, and a front-engine V8 configuration. - Power comes from a 4.3-liter V8, rated at 453 horsepower and 358 lb-ft of torque. item.title: 1989 BMW 325is Coupe
item.description: THIS... is a 1989 BMW 325is Coupe, finished in Avus Blue with a black interior, and offered by Markzuckerberg, a Cars & Bids Certified Seller. - This 325is features the desirable 5-speed manual transmission. - This BMW’s factory-fitted 2.5-liter inline-6 has been swapped with an S52 3.2-liter inline-6. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes a limited-slip differential, a sunroof, and power windows. - A full list of notable modifications reported by the seller is detailed below. Highlights include a VF Engineering supercharger kit, an AEM water/methanol injection system, a Link Engine Management G4+ ECU, a UUC Competition EVO 3 short shift kit, 17-inch Kopi wheels, H&R Race springs, and a Wilwood front big brake kit. - The BMW 325is is a variant of the E30 3 Series, which was produced from 1982 to 1994. The 325is model was known for its sporty performance and was equipped with a more powerful engine than the standard 325i. Its combination of performance, handling, and classic BMW design has made it a favorite among car enthusiasts and collectors. - Power comes from an S52 3.2-liter supercharged inline-6. According to the dyno sheet pictured in the gallery, this BMW now produces 362 horsepower and 296 lb-ft of torque at the wheels as a result of its modifications. It features significant upgrades in power, braking, and handling over the standard Hellcat, and the Widebody configuration further enhances its road presence and cornering capabilities. The Redeye is notable for incorporating technology and design cues from the track-focused Demon, making it a standout option for enthusiasts seeking extreme performance. - Power comes from a 6.2-liter supercharged V8, rated at 797 horsepower and 707 lb-ft of torque in stock form. The 5.2-liter V10 engine was added to the R8 lineup in 2009, and production ended in 2015. - Power comes from a 5.2-liter V10, rated at 525 horsepower and 391 lb-ft of torque.
