item.title: 2002 Chevrolet Silverado 1500 LS
item.description: THIS... is a 2002 Chevrolet Silverado 1500 LS, finished in black with a Graphite interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows that this truck has been registered in Tennessee and Texas since new. - A partial list of notable equipment reported by the seller includes 16-inch wheels, air conditioning, and cruise control. - Notable modifications reported by the seller are limited to a Rugged Liner plastic bed liner, window rain guards, and an aftermarket head unit. - Chevrolet launched the GMT800-based Silverado 1500 for 1999, and it released the Heavy-Duty models for 2001. Every member of the range benefitted from a ground-up redesign that brought updated engines, a more modern look, and comfort-related improvements. - Power comes from a 5.3-liter V8, rated at 285 horsepower and 325 lb-ft of torque. It spins the rear wheels via a 4-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2000 Mazda MX-5 Miata Special Edition
item.description: THIS… is a 2000 Mazda MX-5 Miata, finished in green with a black soft top and a beige and black interior. - This Miata’s factory-fitted 1.8-liter 4-cylinder engine has been swapped with a J32 Type S 3.2-liter V6 engine from a JDM Honda. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report has a few gaps, but shows no mileage discrepancies for the given time period. - Notable modifications reported by the seller are extensive, and a list is detailed below. Highlights include a JDM Honda-sourced J32 Type S 3.2-liter V6 engine swap with a MiniTec JV6 conversion kit, a 5-speed manual transmission swap, an MSD Blaster ignition coil pack, an ACT high-pressure clutch, a lightweight flywheel, an aftermarket soft top, and a Hard Dog M2 Hard Core Double Diagonal roll bar. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes a Torsen limited-slip differential, leather upholstery, and a Bose sound system. - Mazda launched the 2nd-generation MX-5 Miata for the 1999 model year. Called NB internally, it received an updated design, a more powerful engine, and a bigger trunk, among other changes. It was easier to live with on a daily basis but it lost none of its predecessor's agility. Mazda replaced the NB with the 3rd-generation NC model in 2006. - Power comes from JDM Honda-sourced J32 Type S 3.2-liter V6. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 5-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2016 Mercedes-AMG GT S
item.description: THIS… is a 2016 Mercedes-AMG GT S, finished in Designo Diamond White with a brown interior. - The odometer currently displays approximately 18,500 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this AMG GT’s past. It also confirms it’s been registered in Florida and Georgia since new. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes 19-inch front and 20-inch rear AMG wheels, an electronic locking differential, a switchable exhaust system, a fixed glass roof, leather upholstery, power-adjustable heated seats, and a Burmester sound system. Notable modifications reported by the seller include an Apple CarPlay infotainment module, and an aftermarket front-end camera. - The Mercedes-AMG GT debuted for the 2015 model year as the second-ever model developed from the ground up by Mercedes-Benz's AMG performance division. The Mercedes-AMG GT took the front mid-engine approach seen in the SLS AMG that came before and refined it into a smaller, more nimble, and more affordable Porsche 911 rival, complete with V8 power and dramatic coupe styling. - Power comes from a 4.0‑liter twin‑turbocharged V8, rated at 503 horsepower and 479 lb‑ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 7-speed AMG Speedshift dual-clutch automatic transmission.

item.title: 2019 BMW 330i xDrive Sports Wagon
item.description: THIS... is a 2019 BMW 330i xDrive Sports Wagon, finished in black with a black interior. - The attached Carfax history report lists no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this 330i's past. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes the Premium, Executive, and Convenience packages, 17-inch Style 392 wheels, and a panoramic sunroof. The selling dealer reports no notable modifications. - BMW launched the sixth-generation 3 Series for 2012. It was called F30 internally, and the station wagon variant released later was known as the F31. Both models were slightly bigger than their predecessors and offered buyers a much more up-to-date interior. Production ended in 2019. While the sedan returned for a seventh generation, BMW hasn't sold another 3 Series wagon in the United States. - Power comes from a turbocharged 2.0-liter inline-4, rated at 248 horsepower and 258 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels via an 8-speed automatic transmission and BMW's xDrive permanent all-wheel drive system.

item.title: 2003 Volvo V70 2.4T
item.description: THIS... is a 2003 Volvo V70 2.4T, finished in Polar White with an Oak interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this V70’s past. It also shows that it has been registered in Arizona since new. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, this V70 is a 2.4T model, and notable factory equipment includes 16-inch wheels, cloth upholstery, dual-zone automatic climate control, cruise control, and power windows, locks, and door mirrors. - Notable modifications reported by the seller are limited to an aftermarket head unit to cassette player and vinyl upholstery on the driver-side inner door panel. - The Volvo V70 shared its platform with the S60 and S80 sedans. The wagon was offered with front- or all-wheel drive, the latter most commonly seen on the Cross Country. A variety of 4-cylinder and 5-cylinder engines were available, with outputs ranging from 168 to 300 horsepower. - Power comes from a 2.4-liter turbocharged inline-5, rated at 197 horsepower and 210 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the front wheels via a 5-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2024 Range Rover P400 SE
item.description: THIS... is a 2024 Range Rover P400 SE, finished in Varesine Blue with an Inozetek Signal Yellow wrap and a Perlino interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Range Rover’s past. It also shows that this Range Rover has been registered to one owner in Florida since new. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, this Range Rover is a P400 SE model, and notable factory equipment includes the Tow and Shadow Exterior packs, 22-inch Style 5127 diamond turned with Satin Dark Grey contrast wheels, a black contrast roof, Tailgate Event Suite with leather cushions, and a Meridian 3D surround sound system. Notable modifications reported by the seller are limited to the front Range Rover badge removed. - Introduced in late 2021 for the 2022 model year, the L460 is the fifth generation of the Range Rover. An "evolution rather than a revolution," the new Range Rover keeps the traditional design of the L405 while bringing new modern elements to the platform. - Power comes from a 3.0-liter turbocharged and supercharged inline-6, rated at 395 horsepower and 406 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels through an 8-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 1998 BMW M3 Coupe
item.description: THIS... is a 1998 BMW M3 Coupe, finished in Titanium Silver Metallic with a black interior. - This M3 is equipped with the desirable 5-speed manual transmission. - The attached Carfax history report shows that this M3 has been registered to one owner since June 2005. - A build sheet is provided in the gallery, and a partial list of notable equipment reported by the seller includes a limited-slip differential, a sunroof, "Vader" front seats, and a Harman/Kardon sound system. Notable modifications reported by the seller include an Active Autowerke exhaust and clear corner lights. - The E36 generation of the iconic BMW M3 was the first M3 powered by an straight-6 engine. Originally, the United States was not slated to get an M3. After owners famously banded together and demanded one be made, BMW brought the M3 to the U.S. market with a de-tuned and simpler engine than the European M3 due to emissions restrictions. It was a major commercial success for BMW, and it cemented the BMW M brand in the U.S. - Power comes from a 3.2-liter straight-6, rated at 240 horsepower and 236 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 5-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 1999 Mercedes-Benz ML320
item.description: THIS... is a 1999 Mercedes-Benz ML320, finished in black with a gray interior. - This ML320 is a right-hand drive, Japanese-market SUV that's titled and located in North Carolina. The odometer reads in metric units and indicates approximately 56,300 kilometers or roughly 35,000 miles. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes roof rails, leather upholstery, a leather-covered steering wheel and gear shift knob, and automatic climate control. - Notable modifications reported by the seller are limited to 18-inch AMG style wheels and an Alpine head unit. - Mercedes-Benz initially developed the first-generation M-Class as a replacement for the G-Class but the two SUVs ended up coexisting by filling different roles. Called W163 internally, the original ML was more comfortable and more street-focused than the G and it helped set the template for the luxury SUV as we know it today. - Power comes from a 3.2-liter V6 engine, rated at about 215 horsepower and 229 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels via a 5-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2013 Ford Mustang GT/CS Coupe
item.description: THIS… is a 2013 Ford Mustang GT/CS Coupe, finished in Deep Impact Blue with a Dark Charcoal interior. - The odometer currently displays approximately 39,100 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Mustang’s past. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes the California Special and Comfort packages, a 3.15 limited-slip differential, and a Shaker sound system. - Notable modifications reported by the seller include American Muscle wheels, a Roush exhaust system, a Roush hood scoop, Roush quarter window scoops, and a Dynavin head unit. - The Mustang GT/CS, or California Special, is a special edition Mustang that pays homage to the original 1968 model. It combines the power and performance of the Mustang GT with unique styling cues and additional features. - Power comes from a 5.0-liter V8, rated at 420 horsepower and 390 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2005 Ford Excursion Limited 4x4
item.description: THIS... is a 2005 Ford Excursion Limited 4x4, finished in Pueblo Gold Clearcoat Metallic with a black and tan interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report lists no mileage discrepancies in this Ford's past; it also confirms California registration from new. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, this Excursion is a Limited model, and notable factory equipment includes automatic headlights, heated front seats, third-row seats, automatic climate control, heated and power-adjustable side mirrors, and power windows and locks. - Notable modifications reported by the seller are extensive, and the full list is detailed below. Highlights include ARP head studs, a Bullet Proof Diesel EGR cooler, a Bullet Proof Diesel external oil cooler, a Bullet Proof Diesel fuel injection control module, an Edge Products Evolution CTS3 touchscreen tuner, a 4-6-inch Full Traction Suspensions lift kit, and AMP Research powered side steps. - Released for the 2000 model year, the Excursion still stands proud as the only series-produced SUV that manages to make a Chevrolet Suburban look a little small-boned. It was based on the F-250 Super Duty pickup, as its front end suggested, so it could comfortably carry eight passengers and their gear while towing a Tahoe to Lake Tahoe. Production ended after the 2005 model year. - Power comes from a Power Stroke 6.0-liter turbodiesel V8, rated at about 325 horsepower and 560 lb-ft of torque in stock form. Due to the modifications performed to this Excursion, it may produce more power, but a dyno sheet was not provided to confirm. Output is sent to the rear or all four wheels via a 5-speed automatic transmission and a 2-speed transfer case.

item.title: 2003 BMW 325xi Sedan
item.description: THIS... is a 2003 BMW 325xi Sedan, finished in Gray Green Metallic with a Sand Beige interior. - This E46's odometer displays about 76,600 miles. - The attached Carfax history report lists no accidents or mileage inconsistencies in this BMW's past. It also shows that this 325xi has been registered in California and Nevada since new. - A build sheet is provided in the gallery, and a partial list of notable equipment reported by the selling dealer includes 16-inch wheels, a sunroof, heated and power-adjustable front seats, and wood interior trim. The selling dealer reports no notable modifications. - The E46 was the chassis name given to BMW's fourth-generation 3 Series, which was introduced in 1997. The E46 featured aluminum suspension components, benefited from a 50/50 weight distribution, and was 70% more rigid than the outgoing third-generation 3 Series (E36). - Power comes from a 2.5-liter straight-6, rated at 184 horsepower and 175 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the four wheels via a 5-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2016 Ford Mustang GT Coupe Premium
item.description: THIS… is a 2016 Ford Mustang GT Coupe Premium, finished in Magnetic Metallic with an Ebony interior. - The odometer on this Mustang currently displays approximately 39,200 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no mileage discrepancies in this Mustang’s past. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, this Mustang is a Premium model, and notable factory equipment includes the California Special Package, a Shaker Pro 12-speaker sound system, leather and suede upholstery, heated and ventilated front seats, dual-zone automatic climate control, and remote start. The seller reports no notable modifications. - The sixth-generation S550 Mustang was originally introduced in December 2015, featuring a wider and lower body than its predecessor. More importantly, the S550 was the first Mustang to feature an independent rear suspension across the whole model range, which significantly improved the handling and dynamics of the iconic model. Production on the S550 ended in 2023. - Power comes from a Coyote 5.0-liter V8, rated at about 435 horsepower and 400 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2017 Tesla Model S 75
item.description: THIS… is a 2017 Tesla Model S 75, finished in Pearl White Multi-Coat with a black interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no mileage discrepancies in this Tesla's past. It also shows that it has been registered in Southern states since new. - A partial list of notable equipment reported by the seller includes 19-inch Slipstream wheels, LED exterior lighting, an all-glass panoramic sunroof, carbon fiber decor, a 17-inch capacitive touchscreen infotainment system, and Autopilot. The selling dealer reports no notable modifications. - Tesla introduced the Model S, the first car it developed in-house, in 2012. Still available today, it was one of the first cars that proved EVs could be more than overgrown golf carts. The Model S has undergone numerous powertrain and software evolutions during its life cycle, many of which have made their way to older models through over-the-air updates. - Power for this Model S comes from a single electric motor, which draws electricity from a 75-kilowatt-hour lithium-ion battery pack to power the rear wheels with 382 horsepower and 325 lb-ft of torque, according to third-party estimates. In 2017, the Model S 75 received a maximum driving range rating of 249 miles from the EPA

item.title: 2013 BMW 328i Coupe
item.description: THIS… is a 2013 BMW 328i Coupe, finished in Black Sapphire Metallic with a Coral Red interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no mileage discrepancies in this 328i's past. It also shows that this BMW has been registered in California and North Carolina since new. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes the M Sport and Premium packages, Dakota leather upholstery, and heated front seats. Notable modifications reported by the seller are limited to a wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto-capable infotainment system. - Called E92 internally, the last 3 Series coupe made its debut for the 2007 model year. It was longer and wider than its predecessor, and it offered a more extensive list of technology features. Production ended in 2013, and the E92's successor inaugurated the 4 Series nameplate that BMW still uses today. - Power comes from a 3.0-liter inline-6, rated at 230 horsepower and 200 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2024 Rivian R1S Launch Edition
item.description: THIS… is a 2024 Rivian R1S Launch Edition, finished in Rivian Blue with an Ocean Coast interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no mileage discrepancies in this R1S’s past. - A partial list of notable equipment reported by the seller includes 22-inch Sport Bright wheels, a large battery pack, adaptive air suspension, kinetic roll control, heated and ventilated front seats, and folding third-row seats. The seller reports no notable modifications. - The R1S entered production in 2022 as Rivian's second model. Closely related to the R1T pickup, it's an electric SUV with up to seven seats that was developed with the outdoors in mind. It's spacious, and it features a long list of comfort and technology features as well as through-the-road all-wheel drive. - Power comes from four electric motors, which draw electricity from a 135-kilowatt-hour lithium-ion battery pack to zap the four wheels with 835 horsepower and 908 lb-ft of torque. The 2024 R1S earned a maximum driving range estimate of 303 miles from the EPA when equipped with the 22-inch wheels.

item.title: 2011 BMW X5 xDrive35d
item.description: THIS… is a 2011 BMW X5 xDrive35d, finished in Alpine White with a black interior, and offered by longimotorsports, a Cars & Bids Certified Seller. - The attached Carfax history report indicates no mileage discrepancies in this X5's past. It also shows that this is a one-owner SUV, registered in California since new. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, this X5 is an xDrive35d model, and notable factory equipment includes the Cold Weather, Premium, and Technology packages, a panoramic sunroof, and third-row seating. The selling dealer reports no notable modifications. - Called E70 internally, the second-generation X5 was sold in the United States between the 2007 and 2013 model years. It brought larger exterior dimensions, a new suspension system, and an available third-row seat in addition to a host of new technology features. For 2013, the X5 was offered as the 6-cylinder 35i, the diesel 35d, the V8 50i, and X5 M. Production of the E70-generation X5 ended in 2013. - Power comes from a 3.0-liter turbodiesel inline-6, rated at 265 horsepower and 425 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels via a 6-speed automatic transmission and BMW's xDrive permanent all-wheel drive system.

item.title: 2007 Audi S4 Avant
item.description: THIS… is a 2007 Audi S4 Avant, finished in Phantom Black Pearl with an Ebony and Grey interior. - This S4 features the desirable 6-speed manual transmission. - The attached Carfax history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this S4’s past. - According to the Monroney Label provided in the gallery, this S4 is an Avant model, and notable factory equipment includes the Convenience Package, 18-inch wheels, a S4-specific sport suspension, leather and Alcantara upholstery, and heated Recaro front sport seats. Notable modifications reported by the seller are limited to black-finished wheels. - Released for 2006, the B7-based S4 received an updated version of its predecessor's 4.2-liter V8 engine and a suspension system retuned to deliver a sportier driving experience. Visual tweaks set it apart from the A4, but it retained a relatively low-key design. - Power comes from a 4.2-liter V8, rated at about 340 horsepower and 302 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission and Audi's Quattro permanent all-wheel-drive system.

item.title: 2001 BMW Z3 3.0i Roadster
item.description: THIS... is a 2001 BMW Z3 3.0i Roadster, finished in Titanium Silver Metallic with a black soft top and a black interior. - This Z3 features the desirable 5-speed manual transmission, and the odometer currently displays approximately 40,700 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this BMW’s past. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes the Sport Package, a limited-slip differential, heated seats, air conditioning, and cruise control. - Notable modifications reported by the seller include LED low beam headlight bulbs, a Kenwood head unit, Hertz speakers, and a Rockford Fosgate 8-inch amplified box. - The E36/7 Z3 Roadster was the first mass-produced Z-Series car, diversifying the BMW lineup by adding a compact two-seater roadster while pulling components from past BMW models. The front suspension was derived from the E36 sedan, coupled with rear semi-trailing arm suspension from the first-generation E30 M3. The Z3 was available with numerous engine options, from a 1.9-liter inline-4 engine all the way up to the venerable S54 inline-6 found in the 2001 and 2002 M Coupe and Roadster. - Power comes from a 3.0-liter inline-6, rated at 225 horsepower and 214 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 5-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2000 Toyota Crown Athlete V
item.description: THIS… is a 2000 Toyota Crown Athlete V, finished in Crystal White Pearl and silver with a gray interior. - This Crown is a right‑hand drive, Japanese‑market sedan titled in Florida. It’s equipped with a metric instrument cluster, and its odometer displays about 118,100 kilometers, which represents approximately 73,400 miles. - A partial list of notable factory equipment includes 16-inch wheels, power-folding door mirrors, automatic oscillating center vents, cloth upholstery, wood interior trim, automatic dual-zone climate control, and a navigation system. The selling dealer reports no notable modifications. - Toyota introduced the S170‑series Crown Athlete in 1999 as a sportier take on the Crown luxury sedan, pairing relatively aggressive styling with the fantastic turbocharged 1JZ‑GTE straight‑six, and offering a compelling blend of executive comfort and sleeper performance. Production continued through 2007. - Power comes from a 1JZ‑GTE 2.5‑liter turbocharged inline‑six, rated at about 276 horsepower and 268 lb‑ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 4‑speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2013 Porsche Cayenne
item.description:
THIS... is a 2013 Porsche Cayenne, finished in black with a black interior. - This Cayenne features the desirable 6-speed manual transmission. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Porsche’s past. It also shows that this Porsche has been registered in Texas and California since new.
- According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes 21-inch Cayenne SportEdition wheels, a panorama roof, leather upholstery, and a Bose sound system. - Notable modifications reported by the seller include a Remus exhaust system, Brembo brake calipers, a carbon fiber hood, and a tow hitch receiver. - The second-generation Cayenne was introduced for the 2011 model year, refining Porsche's approach to the luxury SUV segment. It offered improved handling and a broader range of technology compared to its predecessor, aiming to balance performance with daily usability. The 2013 model kept the Cayenne competitive among luxury SUVs by integrating advanced features and Porsche's signature driving dynamics. - Power comes from a 3.6-liter V6, rated at 300 horsepower and 295 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2001 Chevrolet Corvette Coupe
item.description: THIS... is a 2001 Chevrolet Corvette Coupe, finished in Millennium Yellow with a black interior. - This Corvette is equipped with the desirable 6-speed manual transmission. - A partial list of notable equipment reported by the seller includes the Z51 Performance Handling Package, a limited-slip differential, 17-inch front and 18-inch rear wheels, a removable roof panel, and a head-up display. The only notable modification reported by the seller is a Hurst shift knob. - Released for the 1997 model year, the fifth-generation Corvette wore a more streamlined exterior design than its predecessor, though it retained the pop-up headlights. It also gained a new V8 engine and a rear-mounted transaxle that improved weight distribution while freeing more space in the cabin. Chevrolet released a convertible model for 1998, and production ended in 2004. - Power comes from a 5.7-liter V8, rated at 350 horsepower and 375 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2010 Range Rover HSE
item.description: THIS… is a 2010 Range Rover HSE, finished in Santorini Black with a Jet interior, and offered by longimotorsports, a Cars & Bids Certified Seller. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no mileage discrepancies in this Range Rover’s past. It also confirms it's been registered in California since new. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, this Range Rover is an HSE model, and notable factory equipment includes an adaptive air suspension, a sunroof, leather upholstery, power-adjustable heated front seats, heated rear seats, a digital instrument cluster, and a Harman Kardon Logic7 sound system. Notable modifications reported by the selling dealer are limited to 22-inch Niche wheels. - Land Rover introduced the third-generation Range Rover for 2003. Called L322 internally, the SUV was developed under BMW's guidance with an eye on luxury and on-road comfort without compromising off-road prowess; it had to fend off competition from a growing list of rivals in the segment it helped create. 2006 brought a series of updates, including Jaguar-sourced V8 engines, and production ended in 2012. - Power comes from a 5.0-liter V8, rated at 375 horsepower and 375 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels via a 6-speed automatic transmission and a 2-speed transfer case.

item.title: 2015 Mercedes-Benz S65 AMG Coupe
item.description: THIS… is a 2015 Mercedes-Benz S65 AMG Coupe, finished in Obsidian Black with a black interior. - The odometer on this S65 currently displays approximately 39,800 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no mileage discrepancies in this S65’s past. It also shows that this S65 Coupe has been registered in California since new. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes the Driver Assistance Package, 20-inch AMG wheels, AMG adaptive sport suspension, a heated steering wheel, and a Burmester sound system. - Notable modifications reported by the seller are limited to a later-model S-Class front grille, orange-finished brake calipers, and black-wrapped exterior trim. - Mercedes-Benz brought a coupe to its S-Class lineup for the first time since the 1990s with the launch of the C217 for the 2014 model year. The AMG-developed S65 was positioned at the top of the range, with both a hard-hitting twin-turbocharged V12 engine and some of the most luxurious creature comforts in the grand touring class. C217 production ended in 2020, and the model wasn't replaced. - Power comes from a 6.0-liter twin-turbocharged V12, rated at 621 horsepower and 738 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 7-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2015 Ford F-350 Super Duty Lariat 4x4
item.description: THIS... is a 2015 Ford F-350 Super Duty Lariat 4x4, finished in White Platinum Metallic with a black interior. - This F-350 Super Duty is a Canadian-spec truck that's titled and located in Florida. The odometer reads in both imperial and metric units and indicates approximately 105,000 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report indicates no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Ford’s past. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, this F-350 Super Duty is a Lariat 4x4 model, and notable factory equipment includes the Platinum and FX4 Off-Road Packages, and a power sunroof. Notable modifications reported by the seller include an aftermarket air intake and exhaust, removed catalytic converters, EGR delete, ECU tune, a Rancho lift kit, and 22-inch Fuel wheels. - Ford unveiled the third-generation F-350 in 2011, overhauling the exterior design, interior appointments, and powertrain, making the trucks not only nicer to drive, but also more capable. The third-gen Super Duty was aimed at dominating the HD work truck category -- to this end, Ford gave it the thickest steel frame in class and introduced the 6.7-liter Power Stroke turbodiesel engine which helped to give the Super Duty the most torque in class as well. - Power comes from a 6.7-liter Power Stroke turbodiesel V8, rated at 440 horsepower and 860 lb-ft of torque in stock form. Due to the modifications performed to this F-350, it may produce more power, but a dyno sheet was not provided to confirm. Output is sent to the rear or all four wheels via a 6-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2016 Volkswagen Golf SportWagen S
item.description: THIS... is a 2016 Volkswagen Golf SportWagen S, finished in Ninja Black with a Titanium Black interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report indicates no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this SportWagen’s past. It also shows that this Volkswagen has been registered to one owner in Florida since new. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, this SportWagen is an S model, and notable factory equipment includes 15-inch wheels, roof rails, a rearview camera, and cruise control. The selling dealer reports no notable modifications. - Rebranded in the U.S. in 2015 from the previous "Jetta SportWagen," the Golf SportWagen caught up to the European naming convention. Boasting 66.5 cubic feet of rear cargo space, the Golf SportWagen offers passenger space that rivals larger SUVs while preserving a lower center of gravity. It also utilizes Volkswagen's successful Modular Transverse Matrix (MQB) architecture, introduced in 2012. - Power comes from a 1.8-liter turbocharged inline-4 engine, rated at 170 horsepower and 199 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the front wheels via a 6-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2020 Alfa Romeo Stelvio Quadrifoglio
item.description: THIS… is a 2020 Alfa Romeo Stelvio Quadrifoglio, finished in Trofeo White with a black and red interior. - This Stelvio's odometer currently displays about 19,100 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report indicates no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Stelvio’s past; it also shows registrations in California from new. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, a partial list of notable factory equipment includes the Carbon Pack, the Active Assist 2 Package, 20-inch wheels, an active suspension system, Brembo brakes, a dual-pane sunroof, heated front seats, and a Harman/Kardon sound system. The seller reports no notable modifications. - The Stelvio was Alfa Romeo's answer to a booming SUV market in North America. It's built on the same Giorgio platform as the Giulia with slight modifications as well as a bump in ride height. The Quadrifoglio — or "four-leaf clover" in Italian — represented the high-performance version of the SUV, powered by a twin-turbo engine developed by Ferrari and related to the automaker's own V8. - Power comes from a Ferrari-derived F154 2.9-liter twin-turbocharged V6, rated at 505 horsepower and 443 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels via an 8-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 1994 Suzuki Cara
item.description: THIS... is a 1994 Suzuki Cara, finished in blue with a black and blue interior. - This Cara is a right-hand drive, Japanese-spec coupe that is titled and located in California. It's equipped with a 5-speed manual transmission and a metric instrument cluster, and the the odometer indicates approximately 186,500 kilometers or roughly 115,900 miles. - A partial list of notable equipment reported by the selling dealer includes a hood scoop, gullwing doors, cloth upholstery, and air conditioning. - Notable modifications reported by the selling dealer include 13-inch Sparco wheels, a Pioneer head unit, and Kenwood speakers. - The Suzuki Cara was the product of a collaboration between Mazda and Suzuki to create a quirky, mid-engine Kei car. The Cara featured gullwing doors, a tiny turbocharged inline-three engine, and a very low weight of around 1,500 lbs. With a driving experience as unique as its aesthetic, the Cara — like the similar Autozam AZ-1 — has become a darling of Japanese Domestic Market car enthusiasts. - Power comes from a turbocharged 657cc inline-3, rated at 63 horsepower and 63 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 5-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2016 Volkswagen Touareg TDI Lux
item.description: THIS… is a 2016 Volkswagen Touareg TDI Lux, finished in Pure White with a Titanium Black interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Volkswagen’s past. It also shows that this Touareg has been registered in Georgia since new. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, this Touareg is a Lux model, and notable factory equipment includes 19-inch wheels, a panoramic sunroof, Vienna leather upholstery, and heated and ventilated front seats. The selling dealer reports no notable modifications. - The second-generation Touareg debuted in 2010 and shared a platform with the Porsche Cayenne of the same era. Known as the 7P internally, it managed to weigh approximately 450 lbs less than its predecessor while packing in more modern technology and safety features and maintaining a reputation for understated comfort and luxury. A facelift was introduced in 2015 and gave the Touareg revised aerodynamics, a retuned suspension, and updated technology. Production ended in 2018. - Power comes from a 3.0-liter turbodiesel V6, rated at 240 horsepower and 406 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels through an 8-speed automatic transmission and Volkswagen's 4Motion permanent all-wheel drive system.

item.title: 1991 Honda NSX
item.description: THIS... is a 1991 Honda NSX, finished in Berlina Black with a black interior. - This NSX is a Japanese-market, right-hand drive coupe that's titled in South Carolina. It's equipped with a metric instrument cluster, and its odometer shows about 32,400 kilometers, which represents approximately 20,100 miles. - A partial list of notable equipment reported by the seller includes 15-inch front and 16-inch rear wheels, leather upholstery, power-adjustable seats, automatic climate control, and cruise control. Notable modifications reported by the seller are limited to adjustable coilovers and aftermarket exterior badges. - Sold as a Honda in global markets, the first-generation Acura NSX made its debut for 1991 as a user-friendly supercar with Ferrari-like performance and Japanese reliability. Aluminum construction kept weight in check, and the NSX stood out as the first production car equipped with titanium connecting rods. Production of the original NSX ended in 2005. - Power comes from a 3.0-liter V6, rated at about 252 horsepower and 210 lb-ft of torque. Output is set to the rear wheels via a 4-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2004 Cadillac Escalade
item.description: THIS… is a 2004 Cadillac Escalade, finished in black with a Shale interior. - This Escalade is a Mexican-market SUV that's currently titled in Texas and equipped with an imperial instrument cluster. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report starts in 2006 but shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies for the given time period. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes leather upholstery, heated front and second-row seats, foldable third-row seats, and 3-zone automatic climate control. Notable modifications reported by the seller are limited to 18-inch Chevrolet wheels. - The Cadillac Escalade quickly rose to prominence as a status symbol of American luxury after its debut in the 1999 model year. The second-generation model based on the GMT800 platform ran from 2002-2006 with more distinct aesthetics than its predecessor and more luxury and tech features. - Power comes from a 5.3-liter V8, rated at 285 horsepower and 325 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 4-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2019 Mercedes-AMG E63 S Wagon
item.description: THIS... is a 2019 Mercedes-AMG E63 S Wagon, finished in Designo Selenite Gray Magno with a Nut Brown and black interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report lists no mileage discrepancies in this Mercedes-AMG's past. - According to the Monroney Label provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes the Driver Assistance, AMG Night, AMG Carbon Fiber, and Warmth & Comfort packages, 20-inch AMG wheels, an AMG Performance exhaust, a panoramic sunroof, heated and ventilated front seats, heated rear seats, and a Burmester sound system. The seller reports no notable modifications. - The AMG E63 S Wagon represents the high-performance variant of the E-Class wagon, combining practicality with advanced engineering from Mercedes-AMG. Introduced as part of the W213 generation, it features all-wheel drive and a powerful twin-turbocharged V8 engine, setting a benchmark for performance wagons. The E63 S Wagon is notable among enthusiasts for its blend of luxury, technology, and exceptional acceleration. - Power comes from a 4.0-liter twin-turbocharged V8, rated at 603 horsepower and 627 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels via a 9-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 1991 Lexus LS 400
item.description: THIS... is a 1991 Lexus LS 400, finished in black with a beige interior. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes 15-inch wheels, a power sunroof, and leather upholstery. The seller reports no notable modifications. - The XF10 was the first generation of the Lexus LS, serving as the brand's first flagship. It marked a turning point in the luxury car world as a Japanese luxury sedan that was truly competitive with more expensive cars from established European competitors. The original Lexus LS has built a well-earned reputation for reliability, value, and long-distance comfort. - Power comes from a 4.0-liter V8, rated at 250 horsepower and 260 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 4-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2016 Porsche Cayman
item.description: THIS... is a 2016 Porsche Cayman, finished in Guards Red with a black interior. - This Cayman features the desirable 6-speed manual transmission, and the odometer currently displays approximately 22,600 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Porsche’s past. It also lists registrations in Oklahoma and California since new. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes the Convenience Package, the Infotainment Package with Bose Surround Sound System, a sport exhaust system, a sport suspension system, and a SportDesign steering wheel. - Notable modifications reported by the seller include 19-inch Apex SM-10 wheels, a Raceseng weighted shift knob, aluminum pedal covers, and a Porsche-crested dead pedal. - Produced from 2012 to 2016, the second-generation Cayman, called 981 internally, was the coupe alternative to the third-generation Boxster. The 981 Cayman was lauded as one of the best-handling sports cars available in its day, largely due to its mid-engine configuration. It was more rigid and lighter than the outgoing 987 Cayman, and it featured a new body, redesigned interior, a wider front track, and a longer wheelbase. - Power comes from a 2.7-liter flat-6, rated at 275 horsepower and 214 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2024 Lotus Emira V6 First Edition
item.description: THIS... is a 2024 Lotus Emira V6 First Edition, finished in Dark Verdant with a tan interior. - This Lotus features a 6-speed manual transmission, and its odometer currently displays approximately 2,200 miles. - The attached Carfax history report lists no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Emira's past. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, this Emira is a V6 First Edition model, and notable factory equipment includes the Full Black Pack, 20-inch diamond-cut forged wheels, sport suspension, heated and power-adjustable sport seats, a 10.25-inch touchscreen infotainment system, and a KEF Uni-Q Premium Audio sound system. The seller reports no notable modifications. - Lotus launched the two-seater Emira for the 2022 model year. The coupe aligns with the brand's famous "light is right" philosophy, thanks in part to a bonded aluminum chassis, and it's offered with either a Toyota-derived supercharged V6 or an AMG-sourced turbocharged 4-cylinder. Light doesn't necessarily mean bare-bones, however, and the Emira is available with more comfort and technology features than earlier Lotus sports cars. Production continues today. - Power comes from a 3.5-liter supercharged V6, rated at 400 horsepower and 310 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2023 Aston Martin Vantage F1 Edition Coupe
item.description: THIS… is a 2023 Aston Martin Vantage F1 Edition Coupe, finished in Lunar White Satin with a Phantom Gray interior. - The odometer on this Vantage currently displays about 7,200 miles. - The attached Carfax history report indicates no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Vantage’s past. - According to the Monroney Label provided in the gallery, this Vantage is an F1 Edition Coupe model, and notable factory equipment includes the F1 Edition package, carbon ceramic brakes, carbon fiber exterior and interior accents, a premium sound system, and a 360-degree camera system. The seller reports no notable modifications. - Aston Martin released the new Vantage for the 2018 model year to replace the outgoing model, which had been on sale since 2006. Much like its DB11 sibling, the Vantage uses Mercedes-Benz's COMAND infotainment system and the AMG M177 twin-turbocharged V8 engine. - The F1 Edition is a more track-focused version of the Vantage developed with input from Aston Martin’s Formula 1 safety car program, featuring increased horsepower, sharper chassis tuning, and added aerodynamic components like a front splitter and fixed rear wing. - Power comes from an AMG-sourced 4.0-liter twin-turbocharged V8, rated at 528 horsepower and 461 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via an 8-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 1979 Pontiac Firebird Trans Am
item.description: THIS... is a 1979 Pontiac Firebird Trans Am, finished in Nocturne Blue with a brown interior. - This Firebird is equipped with the desirable 4-speed manual transmission. - A build sheet details this Firebird's factory equipment. A partial list of notable currently installed equipment reported by the seller includes a Holley carburetor, Competition Engineering subframe connectors, 15-inch Snowflake wheels, removable T-tops, and vinyl upholstery. - The second-generation Firebird made its debut for 1970. It wore sharper-looking styling than the original model and it was offered in numerous configurations, including 6- and 8-cylinder power and several trim levels. It was only available as a coupe, though some models were fitted with removable roof panels. Production ended after the 1981 model year. - Power comes from a 301-cubic-inch V8, rated at about 150 horsepower and 240 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 4-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 1992 Dodge Stealth R/T Turbo
item.description: THIS... is a 1992 Dodge Stealth R/T Turbo, finished in Mystic Blue with a black interior. - This Stealth features a 5-speed manual transmission. - The attached Carfax history report contains some gaps, though it lists no mileage discrepancies for the duration of the report. - According to the seller, notable factory equipment includes 17-inch aluminum wheels, an electronically adjustable suspension system, 4-wheel steering, pop-up headlights, and more as detailed below. - Notable modifications reported by the seller include a catalytic converter delete and an R134a air conditioning refrigerant conversion. - Sold between the 1991 and 1996 model years, the Dodge Stealth was closely related to the Mitsubishi 3000GT, though its exterior design was penned in-house. Like the 3000GT, it was offered with front- or all-wheel drive and with a naturally aspirated or a turbocharged engine. - Power comes from a 6G72 3.0-liter twin-turbocharged V6, rated at 300 horsepower and 307 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels via a 5-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 1994 Toyota Supra Turbo
item.description: THIS... is a 1994 Toyota Supra Turbo, finished in black with a black and blue interior. - This Supra features a 6-speed manual transmission. - This is a right-hand drive, Japanese-spec coupe that's currently titled and located in Texas. It's equipped with a metric instrument cluster, and the odometer currently shows about 94,900 kilometers, or approximately 59,000 miles. - This Supra is a Turbo model. A partial list of notable equipment reported by the seller includes a rear window wiper, dual gloveboxes, power windows, and automatic climate control. - Notable modifications reported by the seller include an HKS air intake system, a Blitz blow-off valve, wastegate, front mount intercooler, and exhaust, 18-inch Volk TE37 wheels, TEIN active-damping suspension, TRD Brembo brakes, carbon fiber front and rear bumpers, a Veilside hood, Recaro front bucket seats, a MOMO steering wheel, aftermarket gauges, and blue interior accents. - Launched in Japan in 1993, the fourth-generation Supra (called A80 internally) was celebrated as one of Toyota's best sports cars even before it earned a starring role in the Fast & Furious franchise. It offered performance that rivaled much more expensive cars and the reliability expected from a Toyota. Production ended in 2002, and the nameplate was consigned to the automotive attic until the BMW-based model arrived in 2019. - Power comes from a 2JZ-GTE 3.0-liter twin-turbocharged inline-6, rated at 276 horsepower and 318 lb-ft of torque in stock form. Due to the modifications performed to this Supra, it likely produces more power, but a dyno sheet was not provided to confirm. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2013 Jaguar XKR Coupe

The XKR gained a supercharger and a more muscular-looking exterior design, among other updates. Production ended in 2014. - Power comes from a 5.0-liter supercharged V8, rated at 510 horsepower and 461 lb-ft of torque. It spins the rear wheels via a 6-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2001 Ford Mustang GT Roush Stage 2 Coupe

Additional notable modifications reported by the seller include a catted X-pipe with Flowmaster Super 40 Series mufflers and a Sony CD player. - A Monroney Label is provided in the gallery, and a partial list of notable equipment reported by the seller includes a limited-slip differential, a power-adjustable driver's seat, and air conditioning. - Introduced for 1994, the SN95 generation of the emblematic Mustang replaced the long-running Fox-based model with a more modern design that incorporated a handful of styling cues borrowed from the original car. It offered enthusiasts better handling, a revised interior, plus a range of V6 and V8 engines. 1999 brought a comprehensively redesigned Mustang nicknamed "New Edge" by enthusiasts, and production ended in 2004. - Power comes from a 4.6-liter V8, rated at 260 horsepower and 302 lb-ft of torque in stock form. Due to the modifications performed to this Mustang, it likely produces slightly more power, but a dyno sheet was not provided to confirm. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a

item.title: 2019 Toyota 4Runner SR5 4x4
item.description: THIS… is a 2019 Toyota 4Runner SR5 4x4, finished in Super White with a black interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this 4Runner’s past; it also indicates registration in Arizona and Florida since new. - According to the copy of the window sticker provided in the gallery, this 4Runner is an SR5 model, and notable factory equipment includes cloth upholstery, a power-adjustable driver's seat, and a power sliding rear hatch window. - A full list of notable modifications reported by the selling dealer is detailed below. Highlights include 18-inch Fuel Off-Road wheels, an aFe air intake system, Fox Racing front coilover struts, and AMP Research power side steps. - Toyota introduced the fifth-generation of the 4Runner for 2010. While many of its rivals moved to a unibody architecture, the 4Runner kept its tried-and-true body-on-frame layout and continued to offer exceptional off-road capabilities. Production ended in 2024, making way for the sixth-gen 4Runner. - Power comes from a 4.0-liter V6, rated at 270 horsepower and 278 lb-ft of torque in stock form. Due to the modifications performed to this 4Runner, it may produce more power, but a dyno sheet was not provided to confirm. Output is sent to the rear or all four wheels via a 5-speed automatic transmission and a 2-speed transfer case.

item.title: 1987 Mercedes-Benz 560SL
item.description: THIS... is a 1987 Mercedes-Benz 560SL, finished in Diamond Blue Metallic with a body-colored hardtop, a dark blue soft top, and a Royal Blue interior. - This 560SL is a Canadian-spec convertible that's registered and located in Quebec, Canada. The odometer reads in metric units and indicates approximately 64,700 kilometers or roughly 40,200 miles. - The attached Carfax history report contains some gaps, though it lists no accidents or mileage discrepancies for the duration of the report. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes 15-inch wheels,wood interior trim, leather upholstery, and automatic climate control. The selling dealer reports no notable modifications. - Mercedes-Benz introduced the R107-generation SL for the 1972 model year in North America, and the roadster remained part of its lineup through 1989. It received several cosmetic and mechanical updates during its unusually long production run, which was the second-longest in the company's history behind the G-Class chassis. Launched in 1986, the 560SL was the ultimate mechanical evolution of the regular-production R107. - Power comes from a 5.5-liter V8, rated at 227 horsepower and 279 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 4-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2011 Audi S4 Prestige
item.description: THIS... is a 2011 Audi S4 Prestige, finished in Phantom Black Pearl Effect with a black interior. - This S4 features the desirable 6-speed manual transmission. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this S4’s past; it also shows Texas, Tennessee, and California registrations since new. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, this S4 is a Prestige model, and notable factory equipment includes the Prestige Package, a sports differential, a power sunroof, and Nappa leather upholstery. - Modifications reported by the seller include an APR supercharger pulley, an Integrated Engineering cold air intake, an APR Stage 2 ECU flash, and a South Bend clutch. - Called B8 internally, the fourth-generation Audi A4 arrived for 2009, and the quicker S4 made its debut for 2010. While it surfed the downsizing wave by swapping its predecessor's V8 for a V6, it offered enthusiasts more torque and quicker acceleration. Engineers gave the Quattro system a rear bias for better handling, and the S4 reflected Audi's upmarket push by offering luxury and tech in spades. - Power comes from a 3.0-liter supercharged V6, rated at 333 horsepower and 325 lb-ft of torque in stock form. Due to the modifications performed to this S4, it may produce more power, but a dyno sheet was not provided to confirm. Output is sent to all four wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission and Audi's Quattro permanent all-wheel drive system.

item.title: 2002 Jeep Wrangler Sahara 4x4
item.description: THIS... is a 2002 Jeep Wrangler Sahara 4x4, finished in Sienna Pearl with a tan hardtop, a tan soft top, and a Camel and Dark Green interior. - This Wrangler features the desirable 5-speed manual transmission, and the odometer currently displays approximately 30,700 miles. - The attached Carfax history report contains a gap between 2008 and 2011, though it lists no accidents or mileage discrepancies for the duration of the report. - According to the seller, this Wrangler is a Sahara model, and notable factory equipment includes 15-inch Canyon wheels, a removable hardtop, full metal doors, and more as detailed below. - Notable modifications reported by the seller are limited to a Bestop soft top and a Jensen Bluetooth receiver. - Round headlights made their first appearance on a Wrangler when the second-generation model arrived in 1996 as a 1997 model. Called TJ internally, it also ushered in a more road-friendly suspension system with coil instead of leaf springs, but it lost none of its predecessor's famed off-road capability. Production ended in 2006. - Power comes from a 4.0-liter inline-6, rated at 190 horsepower and 235 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear or all four wheels via a 5-speed manual transmission and a Command-Trac 2-speed transfer case.

item.title: 2022 Genesis G70 3.3T Sport Prestige
item.description: THIS... is a 2022 Genesis G70 3.3T, finished in Adriatic Blue with a black interior. - The attached Carfax history report lists no accidents in this sedan's past. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, this G70 is a Sport Prestige model, and notable factory equipment includes the Sport Advanced and Sport Prestige packages, heated front seats, and a 10.25-inch display for the infotainment system. The only notable modifications reported by the seller are a K&N Engineering intake system, aftermarket brake rotors, and aftermarket brake pads. - Genesis launched the G70 for 2019 as a legitimate sports sedan to take on established rivals from BMW, Audi, and Cadillac. Standout features include a luxurious interior, great technology, and surprisingly fun handling. An all-wheel drive system for both available engines was offered as an option. - Power comes from a 3.3-liter turbocharged V6, rated at 365 horsepower and 376 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via an 8-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2005 Saab 9-3 Arc Convertible
item.description: THIS... is a 2005 Saab 9-3 Arc Convertible, finished in Silver Metallic with a black soft top and a Slate Grey interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report contains multiple reporting gaps, though it shows no mileage discrepancies during the given time period. It also shows California registrations since new. - A partial list of notable equipment reported by the seller includes 16-inch 10-spoke alloy wheels, a power soft top, leather upholstery, and heated front seats. The seller reports no notable modifications. - The final iteration of the Saab 9-3 was introduced in 2003 and sold as a sedan, SportCombi wagon, and convertible. Safety and handling were key for the new 9-3 with features like Active Head Restraints (SAHR II) to reduce whiplash, as well as ReAxs, a passive rear-wheel steering design, and passive toe-in alignment to help reduce understeer under heavy braking. - Power comes from a 2.0-liter turbocharged inline-4, rated at about 210 horsepower and 221 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the front wheels via a 5-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2023 Dodge Charger SRT Hellcat Widebody Jailbreak
item.description: THIS... is a 2023 Dodge Charger SRT Hellcat Widebody Jailbreak, finished in Plum Crazy with a black interior. - The odometer on this Charger currently displays approximately 3,000 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Charger’s past. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, this Charger is a Hellcat Widebody Jailbreak model, and notable factory equipment includes Customer Preferred Package 23B, the Harman/Kardon Audio Group, 20-inch wheels, and Brembo front brake calipers. The selling dealer reports no notable modifications. - Dodge rebooted the Charger for the 2011 model year with a new exterior design and better visibility. The model remained a full-size, rear-wheel-drive sedan with an available V8; Dodge boldly rejected the downsizing trend sweeping across the car industry. 2015 brought a facelift that ushered in a more muscular-looking design and better interior materials. Charger production ended in late 2023. - Power comes from a 6.2-liter supercharged V8, rated at 717 horsepower and 650 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via an 8-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 1999 Chevrolet Corvette Convertible
item.description: THIS… is a 1999 Chevrolet Corvette, finished in Sebring Silver Metallic with a black soft top and Firethorn Red interior. - The odometer on this Corvette currently displays approximately 28,900 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report contains a reporting gap from 2004 to 2009, though it shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies during the given time period. - According to the Monroney Label provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes 17-inch front and 18-inch rear wheels, a limited-slip differential, an Active Handling System, leather upholstery, a Bose premium sound system, and a head-up display. The selling dealer reports no notable modifications. - Released for the 1997 model year, the fifth-generation C5 Corvette wore a more streamlined design than its predecessor. It also gained a new V8 and a rear-mounted transaxle that improved weight distribution while clearing up more space in the cabin. It was replaced by the C6 for the 2005 model year. - Power comes from an LS1 5.7-liter V8, rated at 345 horsepower and 350 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 4-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 1981 Mercedes-Benz 300TD
item.description: THIS... is a 1981 Mercedes-Benz 300TD, finished in Light Ivory with a Palomino interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report start sin 1999 and contains multiple reporting gaps, though it shows no accidents during the given time period. It also shows California and Washington registrations. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes 14-inch alloy wheels, a sliding sunroof, automatic climate control, and power windows. - Notable modifications reported by the selling dealer are limited to a fuel water separator. - Mercedes-Benz introduced the W123 sedan for 1977 and quickly dropped the gasoline-burning models to focus on the more popular diesel-powered variants. The range included the 240D, which received a 4-cylinder diesel engine, and the 300D, which offered an extra cylinder (and, later, a turbo). Sales ended after the 1985 model year, when the W123 was replaced by the W124 (which later became the first E-Class). - Power comes from an OM617 3.0-liter turbodiesel 5-cylinder engine, rated at 120 horsepower and 170 lb-ft of torque. It spins the rear wheels via a 4-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2003 BMW M3 Coupe
item.description: THIS… is a 2003 BMW M3 Coupe, finished in Carbon Black Metallic with a Caramel interior. - The odometer on this M3 currently displays approximately 78,800 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no mileage discrepancies in this M3’s past. It also shows that this M3 has been registered in Southern and Western states since new. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes the Premium Package, xenon headlights, 19-inch Style 67 wheels, a limited-slip differential, automatic climate control, and a Harman/Kardon sound system. The seller reports no notable modifications. - The third-generation M3 made its debut for the 2000 model year as the quickest and most powerful evolution of the E46 3 Series. Offered as a coupe and as a convertible, it received a high-revving straight-6 engine, suspension improvements, and a muscular-looking exterior design. Production ended in 2006. - Power comes from a S54 3.2-liter straight-6, rated at 333 horsepower and 262 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed SMG automated manual transmission.

item.title: 2018 Porsche Panamera 4S Sport Turismo
item.description: THIS... is a 2018 Porsche Panamera 4S Sport Turismo, finished in Chalk with a black and Saddle Brown interior. - The attached Carfax history report indicates no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Panamera's past. - According to the Monroney Label provided in the gallery, this Panamera is a 4S Sport Turismo model, and notable factory equipment includes the Sport, Assistance, and SportDesign packages, 21-inch Exclusive Design wheels in Jet Black Metallic, LED Matrix headlights in black with Porsche Dynamic Light System Plus, soft-close doors, front and rear heated seats, and ventilated and massaging front seats. Notable modifications reported by the seller are limited to a Pedal Commander throttle response controller. - Porsche's second-generation Panamera arrived for 2017 with a much sleeker-looking design, and it spawned the Sport Turismo (the company's first series-produced station wagon) in 2018, but the updates weren't strictly visual. It was built on a new, aluminum-intensive architecture and it inaugurated a range of 6- and 8-cylinder engines. Production of the second-generation Panamera ended in 2024. - Power comes from a 2.9-liter twin-turbocharged V6, rated at 440 horsepower and 405 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the four wheels via an 8-speed PDK dual-clutch automatic transmission.

item.title: 2001 BMW 540i Sedan
item.description: THIS... is a 2001 BMW 540i Sedan, finished in Jet Black with a black interior. - This 540i has been converted to a 6-speed manual transmission. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this BMW's past. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes the Sport Package, xenon headlights, a sunroof, Montana leather upholstery, power-adjustable front seats with driver memory, and fold-down rear seats. - Notable modifications reported by the seller include a 6-speed manual transmission swap, BC coilovers, Dinan air intakes, a muffler delete, a wheel stud conversion kit, a ZHP shift knob, and an aftermarket head unit. - The E39-generation 5 Series first became available to the US market for the 1997 model year. Available at launch, the V8-powered 540i remained positioned as the quickest and most expensive non-M-badged model until production ended after the 2003 model year. - Power comes from an M62 4.4-liter V8, rated at 282 horsepower and 324 lb-ft of torque when stock. Due to the modifications performed to this 540i, it may produce more power, but a dyno sheet was not provided to confirm. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2013 Mercedes-Benz SL550
item.description: THIS... is a 2013 Mercedes-Benz SL550, finished in Cerussite Grey Magno with a black and red interior. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no mileage discrepancies in this SL550’s past. It also shows that this SL550 has been registered to two owners in Florida since new. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes the Premium 1 and Sport Wheel packages, a power-retractable hardtop, Nappa leather upholstery, and a Harman/Kardon sound system. The seller reports no notable modifications. - Mercedes-Benz launched the R231-generation SL for 2013 with revised styling and a weight saving of about 300 pounds thanks in part to the widespread use of aluminum. The two-seater came standard with a power-operated hardtop, offered buyers a longer list of tech features, and was available with a wide palette of powertrains ranging from a V6 to a V12. Production ended in 2020. - Power comes from a 4.6-liter twin-turbocharged V8, rated at 429 horsepower and 516 lb-ft of torque. It spins the rear wheels via a 7-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 2000 Cadillac Seville STS
item.description: THIS... is a 2000 Cadillac Seville STS, finished in Sable Black with a black interior. - The odometer currently displays approximately 19,500 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no mileage discrepancies in this Seville’s past. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, this Seville is an STS model, and notable factory equipment includes 16-inch wheels, a power sunroof, leather upholstery, heated front and rear seats, and cruise control. Notable modifications reported by the seller include a Pioneer touchscreen head unit and a rearview camera. - Cadillac launched the fifth and final generation of the Seville for the 1998 model year. Still positioned at the top of the firm's range, the sedan was available in two variants called Seville Luxury Sedan (SLS) and Seville Touring Sedan (STS), respectively; this is the latter. Equipped with sportier suspension and grippier seats than the SLS, the "Touring" was the one to have if backroad carving was on the menu. Production ended in 2003, though sales continued into 2004. - Power comes from a 4.6-liter Northstar V8, rated at 300 horsepower and 295 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the front wheels via a 4-speed automatic transmission.

item.title: 1999 Volvo C70 Coupe
item.description: THIS... is a 1999 Volvo C70 Coupe, finished in Mystic Silver Metallic with a grey interior. - This Volvo features the desirable 5-speed manual transmission. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report contains multiple reporting gaps, though it shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies during the given time period. It also shows that this C70 has been registered in California since new. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes a power sunroof, leather upholstery, heated front seats, automatic climate control, and an AM/FM/CD/cassette stereo. - A list of notable modifications reported by the seller is detailed below. Highlights include Wiseco forged engine pistons, SP Components forged connecting rods, a K24 turbocharger, engine assembly balanced and honed, ported cylinder heads, a Hilton Tuning Stage 3 ECU tune, a Quaife differential, a TME header-back exhaust system, 17-inch Pegasus wheels, and headlights from a later C70. - The first-generation Volvo C70 debuted in 1998 as a stylish grand touring coupe based on the 850 platform. Developed in partnership with TWR (Tom Walkinshaw Racing), it featured a more refined suspension and sleeker design than the sedan it was based on. The C70 offered turbocharged five-cylinder engines and a choice of manual or automatic transmissions, blending performance with Volvo's signature safety focus. - Power comes from a 2.3-liter turbocharged inline-5, rated at 236 horsepower and 244 lb-ft of torque. Due to the modifications performed to this C70, it may produce more power, but a dyno sheet was not provided to confirm. Output is sent to the front wheels via a 5-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2009 Porsche Cayenne GTS
item.description: THIS… is a 2009 Porsche Cayenne GTS finished in GTS Red with a black interior. - This Cayenne GTS features the desirable 6-speed manual transmission. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report shows no mileage discrepancies in this Cayenne’s past. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes 21-inch Cayenne Sport wheels, bi-xenon headlights, an enlarged rear spoiler, a panoramic sunroof, leather and Alcantara upholstery, power-adjustable and heated front seats, and heated rear seats. Notable modifications reported by the selling dealer are limited to the wheels refinished in black. - The first-generation Cayenne made its hugely controversial American debut for the 2003 model year, and it quickly won over skeptics with its unusual blend of on-road performance and off-road prowess. It was updated for 2008 with a new-look design and more technology, and it retired as the best-selling member of the Porsche range in 2010. - Power comes from a 4.8-liter V8, rated at 405 horsepower and 369 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2024 Tesla Model 3 Performance
item.description: THIS... is a 2024 Tesla Model 3 Performance, finished in Quicksilver with a black interior. - The odometer on this Model 3 currently displays about 7,700 miles. - The attached Carfax vehicle history report indicates no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Tesla’s past. - According to the build sheet provided in the gallery, notable factory equipment includes 20-inch Warp wheels, a Premium interior, and Full Self Driving (Supervised). The seller reports no notable modifications. - Positioned as Tesla's entry-level car, the Model 3 was celebrated as a premium EV with a usable range and respectable performance, all at a reasonably attainable price. It's still available today, and it has gone through several evolutions (including many software updates) since its introduction. A refresh for the 2024 model year brought updated lights, more power, an all-new premium interior, and a quieter ride. - Power comes from dual electric motors that draw electricity from a 75-kilowatt-hour lithium-ion battery pack to power all four wheels with 510 horsepower and 554 lb-ft of torque, according to third-party estimates. In 2024, the Model 3 Performance received a maximum driving range rating of 303 miles from the EPA.

item.title: 2014 Chevrolet Camaro 1LS
item.description: THIS… is a 2014 Chevrolet Camaro 1LS, finished in Blue Ray Metallic with a black interior. - This Camaro features the desirable 6-speed manual transmission, and the odometer currently displays approximately 38,100 miles. - The attached Carfax history report indicates no mileage discrepancies in this Camaro’s past. - According to the window sticker provided in the gallery, this Camaro cost $24,600 new. A partial list of notable factory equipment includes a Stabilitrak traction control system, power-reclining front seats, and cruise control. - Notable modifications reported by the selling dealer include a DSX flex-fuel kit and ECU tune by Overkill Motorsports, Camaro Z/28 suspension and chassis bushings with Hotchkis rear anti-roll bars, upgraded Camaro SS brake components, 20-inch Camaro SS wheels, ZL1 Addons front & rear rock guards, and a JVC head unit. - Chevrolet brought the Camaro nameplate back after a long hiatus for 2010. Previewed by a concept in 2006, the fifth-generation model wore a heritage-laced exterior design that borrowed some styling cues from the original car, and it received an independent rear suspension system. It remained a pony car at heart, its main rival was the Ford Mustang, but it was also aimed at Japanese coupes like the Nissan 370Z. - Power comes from a 3.6-liter V6, rated at about 323 horsepower and 276 lb-ft of torque in stock form. Due to the modifications performed to this Camaro, it may produce more power, but a dyno sheet was not provided to confirm. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission.

item.title: 2024 Mercedes-AMG GLS 63
